skill.name: develop-frontend
skill.description: Guides Next.js and React frontend implementation, including UI components, API integration, state handling, tests, and local quality checks.
skill.summary: Build structured Next.js and React frontend features with tests and validation steps.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command patterns, two localhost URLs, weak cryptography strings, and high entropy. Review found the command findings are Markdown examples for legitimate frontend work, the URLs are local development references, and the crypto and entropy alerts are false positives. The skill is publishable with a warning because it instructs agents to edit project files and run local development commands.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate External Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local commands such as git branch checks, component listing, lint, tests, builds, and a development server. These commands support normal frontend work, but they require normal user review because they execute in the project environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible in Markdown code fences and match the skill purpose. I found no evidence of shell obfuscation, secret access, destructive commands, or remote payload execution.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to localhost development pages used for manual browser verification. They are not external exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs use localhost and appear inside instructions for starting or navigating a local development server. No evidence found of outbound data transfer to a third-party service.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and high entropy alerts map to normal Markdown frontmatter, Japanese prose, and TypeScript test examples. Review found no cryptographic implementation and no binary or encrypted payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text and a test describe block, not crypto code. The single Markdown file is readable prose, so the entropy alert is best explained by mixed Japanese and technical content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project File Editing and Shell Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill expects agents to read, write, edit, search, run local checks, and start a development server. This is appropriate for a coding skill but should run only in a trusted repository.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The operational instructions are explicit and directly tied to development validation. The risk is environmental impact, not confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need consistent implementation steps for React pages, components, API hooks, and tests. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured Next.js feature delivery with validation checks.
actual_capabilities: Plans Next.js App Router pages and frontend file placement.
Separates presentational components, container views, and custom hooks.
Guides API integration with generated clients and loading, error, and data states.
Defines testing expectations for Vitest, Testing Library, and MSW.
Requires lint, test, build, and local server validation before completion.
Provides frontend quality rules for TypeScript, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and accessibility.
limitations: It is tailored to a MovieMarketer-style project structure and may need adaptation.
It does not include reusable code files or scripts beyond instructions.
It assumes the repository already uses Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui.
It instructs local command execution, so users should run it only in trusted workspaces.

use_cases.title: Implement Product Screens
use_cases.description: Create a new authenticated frontend page with components, hooks, API calls, tests, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery
use_cases.description: Apply one workflow for branch checks, file structure, component rules, testing, and completion reporting.

use_cases.title: Review Frontend Quality
use_cases.description: Check React and Next.js work against project rules for typing, accessibility, unused code, and build health.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for one presentational component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop-frontend skill to create a reusable React component for this UI requirement: [describe requirement]. Follow the project component structure and add a focused test.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Feature Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for a Next.js page and view layer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop-frontend skill to implement a Next.js App Router page for [feature]. Include the page component, container view, presentational components, and loading or error states.

prompt_templates.title: Connect an API Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for data fetching and state handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop-frontend skill to connect [screen] to the generated API client. Create custom hooks with loading, error, and data states, then add MSW-backed tests.

prompt_templates.title: Deliver a Full Frontend Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for implementation, validation, and report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the develop-frontend skill to complete issue [number] for [feature]. Check branch rules, implement the frontend, run lint, tests, build, start the dev server, and prepare the completion report.

output_examples.input: Add a user profile page with editable fields.
output_examples.output: A completed frontend change with a Next.js page, profile view, form component, validation hook, API integration, tests, and a validation summary.

output_examples.input: Review this dashboard component for project rule compliance.
output: A findings list covering component boundaries, TypeScript strictness, shadcn/ui usage, accessibility, and test gaps.
A short set of recommended fixes ordered by implementation risk.

output_examples.input: Wire the generated client into the campaign list screen.
output_examples.output: A custom data hook, connected container view, loading and error states, MSW test coverage, and local verification notes.

best_practices: Run the skill in a trusted repository with version control enabled.
Provide the issue number, feature name, API contract, and target route before implementation.
Review generated file edits and command output before merging changes.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in repositories that do not follow the expected Next.js structure.
Skipping lint, test, build, or browser validation after code changes.
Allowing broad UI refactors when the task only needs a small feature change.

faq.question: Does this skill write frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides an agent through implementing Next.js and React files when the tool has file editing access.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js App Router, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Vitest, Testing Library, and MSW.

faq.question: Can it work outside the MovieMarketer project?
faq.answer: Yes, but some paths and conventions may need adjustment for another repository.

faq.question: Does it require command execution?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes local checks such as lint, tests, builds, and a development server.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it performs normal coding tasks that can change files and run commands.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The only URL references reviewed are localhost development URLs.
